items.title: Efforts to secure buyer for Liberty Bell Bay smelter ongoing after window for offers closes
items.summary: The window for non-binding offers to purchase for the Liberty Bell Bay smelter, in Tasmania's north, has officially closed, with a union delegation taking their cause to Canberra as workers anxiously await news.

items.title: Study finds link between air pollution exposure and risk of ending up in hospital
items.summary: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

items.title: Homes Tasmania might come and go before Felicity gets a rental
items.summary: Felicity Hirst says she is not too picky about what kind of house she lives in; she just wants one. She has been on Tasmania's social housing list for three and a half years — as long as the state's housing authority has existed.

items.title: Eurovision begins amid boycotts and massive police operation in Vienna
items.summary: This week, the Eurovision Song Contest will burst onto the stage in Vienna, and onto screens around the globe, but behind the colour and campness, this year's competition is shaping up as one of the most politically charged in history.

items.title: Trump and Xi's Beijing summit must confront the AI cold war
items.summary: When Trump last visited China in 2017, artificial intelligence was not yet the centre of global power. Now it is.

items.title: Cricket Australia hopes Big Bash League privatisation could make it one of sport's 'grand slams'
items.summary: Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg says private investment in the Big Bash League is "inevitable" and waiting for too long could mean "we've missed the boat" as more competitions pop up around the world.

items.title: Vanuatu's cabinet approves new version of Nakamal Agreement with Australia
items.summary: The ABC can reveal that after months of difficult negotiations, the Nakamal Agreement has been approved by Vanuatu's cabinet but omits key limitations on China's influence.

items.title: More or Less quiz: Guess where your money goes, and what it gets spent on
items.summary: A new quiz format? In this economy? Celebrate budget day with our More or Less challenge.

items.title: Why Chalmers will rein in the property free-for-all in the federal budget
items.summary: After being rejected twice by an electorate that didn't want to give up lucrative tax arrangements on property, the Albanese government has sniffed the breeze and decided the time is ripe for change.

items.title: The caring burden for single, childless women
items.summary: Single women over 45 without children are much more likely to be spending time caring for family members who are aging, have a disability or chronic illness. And it's not just costing them financially.

items.title: How Mortal Kombat II brought Hollywood to the Gold Coast
items.summary: The $110 million Mortal Kombat sequel is the next in a laundry list of productions putting Queensland on the radar for international films.

items.title: Keir Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong as UK Labour MPs demand his exit
items.summary: The British prime minister is fighting to fend off a growing threat to his leadership after a disastrous set of local election results last week.

items.title: US, Iran reach stalemate in war negotiations dashing hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he's aiming for "complete victory" in Iran as the US and Iran reach an impasse in negotiations.

items.title: Man charged with White House correspondents' dinner shooting pleads not guilty
items.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, has been charged with attempting to assassinate US president Donald Trump at the April dinner.

items.title: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume promises new Coalition after Farrer by-election loss
items.summary: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume is promising a new day for a Coalition that has lost its way, but whether the party can recover from the wreckage of Farrer remains to be seen.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Hadley's shock return 10 weeks post-baby
items.summary: Paige Hadley made a shock return to the Super Netball league less than three months after giving birth to her baby boy. Despite some commentary online questioning her priorities, the midcourter is more determined than ever to prove women can pursue multiple dreams.

items.title: 'Carrot hazard' slows traffic on Tasmanian highway as police seek driver
items.summary: Thousands of carrots have spilled onto a Tasmanian highway, causing a traffic hazard, with police calling for public help to find the driver involved.

items.title: ICC confirms arrest warrant for Philippine Senator Ronald Dela Rosa over war on drugs
items.summary: Video footage displayed during the Senate session showed Philippine senator Ronald Dela Rosa running away from Philippine agents as the International Criminal Court confirmed an arrest warrant had been issued.

items.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Magistrate questions strength of case against teen accused of setting alight Cairns early learning centre
items.summary: A magistrate has told the court a "strong case couldn't be confidently asserted" against a 19-year-old accused of setting fire to a Cairns early learning centre.

items.title: Man charged with disturbing place of worship after allegedly making threats at north Brisbane mosque
items.summary: Masjid Taqwa's board said the alleged incident caused fear and distress among members of the congregation.

items.title: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe found guilty of murder of Dale McKenna in Qld car crash
items.summary: Three years ago, Dale Aaron McKenna was pursued down a dark country road at 180km/h, dying after his ute rolled. Now, the man chasing him has been convicted of his murder.

items.title: Alleged Russian spies Kira and Igor Korolev to face fresh espionage conspiracy charge
items.summary: Australian citizens Kira and Igor Korolev were charged in 2024, accused of attempting to gather and send state secrets to Russia.

items.title: 'Closure': Police believe body found in NSW is alleged triple murderer
items.summary: A body has been found near Lake Cargelligo, where three people were shot and killed in January this year, sparking a massive manhunt.

items.title: Calls about One Nation's electoral chances in Western Sydney 'premature', analyst says
items.summary: One Nation's Barnaby Joyce says potential candidates for Western Sydney were first and second-generation Australians, but an analyst calls him a "great media performer".

items.title: Drink, drug driver David Berry jailed for more than a decade over motorcyclist death
items.summary: A County Court judge has sentenced a drink, drug driver to 11 years' jail after the man hit and killed a motorcyclist while behind the wheel of a stolen ute in Central Victoria.

items.title: Josh Rachele urges fans to 'choose kindness' after receiving abusive message online
items.summary: Adelaide Crows footballer Josh Rachele has called out fan behaviour after receiving an abusive message online amid an ongoing conversation on mental health in the AFL.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship passengers to quarantine in Perth
items.summary: Australian passengers who were on board the hantavirus-hit cruise ship will be ordered to quarantine in Perth for at least three weeks.

items.title: Aboriginal man expresses remorse after avoiding jail for vandalising culturally sensitive site
items.summary: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.

items.title: NT Police review clears officer who pepper sprayed camera operator during Alice Springs riot
items.summary: Northern Territory Police have reviewed an incident in which an officer pepper sprayed a Network 10 camera operator during rioting in Alice Springs last month, concluding his actions were "not inappropriate".

items.title: NDIS provider avoids jail for defrauding scheme in bid to keep business 'afloat'
items.summary: An Adelaide man has avoided jail for making a series of fraudulent claims with the NDIS in a bid to help the struggling business he was working for survive.

items.title: Cafe-worthy breakfast and brunch recipes for relaxed mornings
items.summary: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.

items.title: Hasrat Gill stumbled upon the T20 World Cup on TV one day. It changed her life forever
items.summary: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.

items.title: First Nations man dies in custody at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre in Far North Queensland
items.summary: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.

items.title: Hitchhiking northern quoll released back into wild in central Queensland
items.summary: Members of the public spotted the marsupial fleeing the wheel arch of a truck in Brisbane and alerted authorities.

items.title: Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigns amid Paradise Dam controversy
items.summary: The ABC understands Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigned on Friday after almost six years in the position.

items.title: Son of drug smuggler denies helping dad with Newcastle cocaine importation scheme
items.summary: A man accused of helping his father carry out a drug importation operation in Newcastle tells a jury his dad led a double life.

items.title: Queensland's beleaguered DNA lab clears backlog of hundreds of sexual assault kits, director says
items.summary: Forensic Science Queensland was the subject of two commissions of inquiry, over failures in testing and cultural issues which may have led to miscarriages of justice.

items.title: Australian Defence Force Academy cadet charged after allegedly using hidden bathroom camera to film women
items.summary: An Australian Defence Force Academy cadet who allegedly used a camera hidden in a soap dispenser to film female cadets in the bathroom has been granted bail in the ACT Magistrates Court.

items.title: Police investigate reports of hundreds of pregnant sheep stolen from Great Southern farm
items.summary: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.

items.title: Jewish teen subjected to antisemitic slurs on Minecraft, royal commission told
items.summary: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears a 15-year-old was subjected to antisemitic slurs while playing the popular video game with students from a Perth school.

items.title: Western Australia will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050 on current trajectory, Woodside-funded report warns
items.summary: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

items.title: Victoria to fund free meningococcal B vaccines for Year 10 students
items.summary: The government will fund a $9 million program to vaccinate students against meningococcal B from January 1 next year.

items.title: Trees razed as North Adelaide golf course redevelopment gets underway
items.summary: Workers have begun cutting down trees at the North Adelaide Golf Course as part of a controversial redevelopment at the site.

items.title: Afghanistan's women's cricket team urges ICC to replicate FIFA recognition
items.summary: Afghanistan's exiled women cricketers are urging the International Cricket Council (ICC) to follow FIFA's lead in recognising them as a legitimate national team.

items.title: Crocodile wrangler Matt Wright released from prison, missed baby boy's birth while behind bars
items.summary: Outback Wrangler star Matt Wright has served five months in a Darwin prison, revealing he was not released in time for the birth of his third child.

items.title: Traffic camera captures 'spectacular' meteor breaking up over Tasmania
items.summary: Tasmanians across the state reported seeing the bright lights of a meteor last night. Astronomer Martin George says the spectacle was typical of a natural object burning up in the atmosphere.

items.title: Woman killed in Mother's Day crash on the Gold Coast
items.summary: A 41-year-old woman has died after a horror Mother's Day crash in the Gold Coast hinterland that left two others critically injured.

items.title: Court to rule if Andrew Forrest will have to compensate the Yindjibarndi for mining their land
items.summary: Tomorrow will mark a historic day for an outback WA community as the Federal Court decides how much compensation it is due after Andrew Forrest's FMG mined its land without permission.

items.title: Ambulance Victoria condemns assault on pregnant paramedic in Melbourne
items.summary: A pregnant paramedic has been assaulted in Melbourne less than a fortnight after the stabbing of a colleague, the union says.

items.title: Lucas Herbert secures maiden LIV win despite final-round jitters
items.summary: Australian Lucas Herbert is off to the US Open after scoring a nerve-shredding four-shot victory at LIV Golf Virginia in Washington DC.

items.title: Knox Family brings country, rock and stories to Sydney Opera House for 'Generations and Dynasties'
items.summary: Uncle Roger Knox, aka the Black Elvis, will perform a collection of his hit songs and a few yarns from his life at the Sydney Opera House, sharing the stage with his son and two grandsons.

items.title: Two mums on having babies in their 40s
items.summary: Whether it's about overcoming health challenges, waiting to meet "the right person" or wanting to be financially stable, Australian women are increasingly having children later in life.

items.title: Australian flags flown upside down as protesters try to 'make Victoria great again'
items.summary: Unhappy Victorians are flying upside-down Australian flags in political protest, but what exactly does it mean, and what do their neighbours think?

items.title: Arsenal beats West Ham thanks to VAR call with massive implications at top and bottom of EPL ladder
items.summary: Another late goal and a controversial VAR call have helped Arsenal stay in control of the Premier League title race, while delivering another blow to relegation-threatened West Ham United.

items.title: This is what experts say you should not include in your resume
items.summary: Here's what three experts recommend you don't put in your resume for the best chance of securing an interview or landing a role.

items.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City beats Sam Kerr's Chelsea in FA Cup semifinal, Dario Vidošić's Brighton beats Liverpool
items.summary: Mary Fowler's goal kick-started Manchester City's FA Cup comeback to down Sam Kerr's Chelsea and set up a final against Brighton, led by former Socceroo Dario Vidošić.

items.title: Bondi royal commission to hear challenge to antisemitism definition, quiz UK experts
items.summary: A royal commission sparked by the Bondi terror attack is this week expected to hear from groups who challenge the commission's accepted definition of antisemitism, as well as expert witnesses from the United Kingdom.

items.title: Queensland weather to bring widespread 'nuisance' showers and 'unseasonable' rain in the west
items.summary: The weather bureau says cooler morning temperatures in Queensland have been put on pause, with scattered showers expected across the week.

items.title: Authorities evacuate passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Antarctic ship Nuyina's refuelling saga unresolved as deadline looms, documents show
items.summary: A costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker might not be resolved for another three years under most options, internal government documents reveal.

items.title: Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Test between Wallabies and All Blacks can be a major win
items.summary: Australian rugby lacks a marquee event that captures the national imagination each year, but this could change if a bold Bledisloe Cup proposal gets approval.

items.title: Confined 17 exhibition showcases art of incarcerated First Nations women
items.summary: A day after her 18th birthday, Flick Chafer-Smith hit rock bottom when she was sent to jail. She found salvation through art.

items.title: Inside the course targeting young tradies with get-rich schemes
items.summary: "Escape the 9-5" content is blowing up on social media, but people who have gotten caught up in it warn of the costs.

items.title: Aged care patients stranded in hospitals prompt calls for government action
items.summary: The number of aged care patients stuck in hospitals has jumped by 35 per cent in less than a year, fuelling fresh demands for the federal government to stem the crisis.
